page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.n1.large is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY processor. It features 4 physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The memory configuration consists of 8.0 GB of RAM, providing 2.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on external storage options.

Benchmark results place the ecs.n1.large in the lower performance tiers for most workloads. Its stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance scores of 1106.18 and 4400.61, respectively, fall into the bottom 25% tier. Memory performance is similarly constrained, with read-only large block bandwidth at 34.59 GB/sec, although large block write bandwidth achieves an average rating at 29.39 GB/sec. Cryptographic and compression tests, such as gzip single-threaded compression and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, also register in the lower percentiles.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation but modest performance profile, the ecs.n1.large offers an entry-level option for workloads requiring dedicated resources without high memory or storage demands. It is suitable for basic web serving, light database operations, and development or testing environments that do not require high-throughput processing or extensive memory capacity.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.n1.large is an x86_64 server instance featuring a dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY processor with 4 physical cores, 4 vCPUs, and a 2.5 GHz clock speed. Equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM, it does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate a lower-tier profile, with stress-ng CPU and memory read bandwidth scores falling in the bottom 25% of tested instances, though large block write bandwidth remains average. The instance supports AVX and AVX-512F instruction sets. Given its dedicated CPU allocation and modest resource density, the ecs.n1.large is best suited for entry-level workloads such as basic web serving, lightweight database operations, and development or testing environments that do not demand high memory or intensive computational throughput.

tagline: A dedicated entry-level compute instance featuring four physical cores and balanced resources for lightweight, non-intensive workloads.
